data.body:
G4 (child consistency) container review — parent epic `5fd2` × child `1913` (Pass-1 finder): NO FINDINGS (clean pairing).

Audited all 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child against the WHOLE sibling roster; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied. Each mode resolves consistent-by-design.

1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — none unowned. Pass-1's findings flow into a shared pipeline, but the shared artifact (the per-finding `id`) is explicitly NOT minted by Pass-1: ca03 is the SOLE owner (`mint_finding_id`, stamped at result assembly); 1913's `P1Finding` schema correctly carries no `id` field, and 75a9/db7b CONSUME the id (reference-not-restate). No two children both write the id; no hidden coupling.

2. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — examined the most plausible candidate and it is DESIGNED, not contradictory: 1913 frames Pass-1 as "skeptical-of-plan / grounding-not-charity / thorough (precision is Pass-2's job)," while sibling acc1 (Pass-2) frames itself as "charitable-plan-reading (the finding stands only if it holds under the plan's most reasonable reading)." These are the two halves of the parent's deliberate division of labor (parent: "Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS ... precision is Pass-2's job"; "Pass 2 ... charitable plan-reading"). Each child states its own half correctly — complementary, not in conflict. Benign-reading filter: PASS.

3. DEPENDENCY GAP — none. The Pass-1→Pass-2→Pass-3 ordering is coherent: acc1 explicitly takes Pass-1's evidence records as claims-to-test; 487d consumes Pass-2's attributes/sub-answers; ca03 orchestrates the sequence. No missing sequencing edge for 1913.

4. SCOPE OVERLAP — none. 1913's added coaching-spec AC ("findings conform to the coaching spec: grounded in criterion+location, specific+actionable, affirm-what-passes, checklist line, suggested_fix when confident") is the FINDING-AUTHORING half of the parent's "Feedback-authoring conforms to the coaching spec" AC and is DISTINCT from sibling 75a9's Pass-4 affirmative-COACH (the forward-MOVE layer over surviving findings). The parent enumerates these as two separate ACs; 1913 owns finding-authoring, 75a9 owns move-coaching. No double-ownership.

5. ORDERING VIOLATION — none. Per the parent's risk-ordered MVP build order, 1913 is in the thin vertical slice (DET floor 012e + registry/orchestrator ca03 + ONE single-turn criterion + Pass-1/2/3 1913/acc1/487d + attestation 4bb7 + claim 092b). Pass-1 precedes Pass-2/3, which is the correct dependency order.

6. CONSUMER IMPACT — none. 1913's emit-schema field set (post-remediation: `{finding, criteria, location, evidence, scenarios, impact, checklist_item, suggested_fix}`) is consumed downstream without contradiction: acc1 treats findings field-agnostically (claims-to-test); ca03 stamps the `id`; db7b's `_slim` records `id/criteria/tier/decision/severity/validity/impact/priority/reason/verification` — it does not depend on Pass-1's location/checklist fields, so the added coaching fields do not break any consumer. The parent's two-place statement of the Pass-1 contract (bare `{finding,criteria,evidence,scenarios,impact}` in the Pass-1 spec vs. the fuller coaching-spec finding shape) is reconciled by 1913 carrying the union — consistent with the parent's coaching-authoring AC, not a contradiction.

7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — none. 1913 references no removed/renamed sibling artifact; the finder is a library prompt (`plan_review_finder.md`) resolved via the shipped da27 prompt machinery (ca03's lane), with no dangling pointer.

NET: clean for G4 — this child is consistent with the parent and every sibling it interacts with across all 7 modes; the one examined near-candidate (skeptical-vs-charitable Pass-1/Pass-2 framing) is an intentional, parent-specified division of labor. Findings list is EMPTY.
